subject: Statistics
title: MCMC: The Engine of Modern Bayes
description: A quick win: understand MCMC: The Engine of Modern Bayes.

hook.text: How do you explore a mountain range in the dark? Walk randomly, but step uphill more often than down.

buildup.text: MCMC — Markov Chain Monte Carlo — explores probability landscapes by taking clever random walks.

discovery.text: It generates samples from the posterior distribution without solving impossible integrals.

twist.text: MCMC was invented in the 1940s for nuclear physics at Los Alamos. It took decades to reach statistics.

climax.text: Without MCMC, Bayesian statistics would still be a beautiful theory nobody could use. It's the key enabler.

punchline.text: Random walks that converge to truth. That's MCMC.

quiz.question: What does MCMC do in Bayesian statistics?
options: It generates samples from the posterior distribution without solving intractable integrals
It proves which hypothesis is correct
It replaces the need for data collection
